_meta.extracted_by: whole-word match over baked WEB · curated by claude-opus-4-8 · 2026-07-03
_meta.scope: Distinctive persons & places, each scoped to the books where its name is unambiguous (Torah + OT narrative & prophets).
_meta.limitations: Exact-name match only, no coreference or pronoun anaphora. Each entity is confined to a curated `books` scope, so recall is deliberately bounded (a figure named outside its scope gets no chip) in exchange for precision. Ambiguous common names the seed cannot safely disambiguate — most of the New Testament (John, Mary, Simon, James, Judas), tribe-vs-person eponyms outside the Torah — are LEFT for the frontier sweep over all 66 books (Session 2), not approximated here. A verified seed, not the sweep.
_meta.contested_policy: Where a tradition disputes an identity or reading, the entity carries a `contested` block with the competing views and their sources. The app never presents one lens as neutral fact.

entities.summary: The garden God planted eastward, where man was set.
The mountains where the ark came to rest.
Where the LORD confused the one language of the earth.
Ur of the Chaldees, Abram's birthplace.
Where Terah settled and Abram was called onward.
The land promised to Abraham and his seed.
Abram's first altar-site in the land.
'House of God'; Jacob's ladder and vow.
Where Abraham pitched his tent and was buried.
'Well of the oath'; a patriarchal boundary.
The city of the plain destroyed with Gomorrah.
Destroyed with Sodom by fire from heaven.
Melchizedek's city, later associated with Jerusalem.
The land of bondage and of Joseph's exaltation.
The region of Egypt where Israel dwelt.
Where Moses fled and kept Jethro's flock.
The mountain of the Law and the covenant.
The mountain of God; the burning bush and the rock.
The river bounding the land of promise.
The plains where Israel camped before crossing over.
The height from which Moses saw the land and died.
The wilderness camp of Israel's long sojourn.
The city of palms whose walls fell at the shout.
Where the tabernacle stood and Samuel was called.
Israel's first camp across the Jordan.
The mount of Elijah's contest with the prophets of Baal.
Capital of the northern kingdom of Israel.
The great city of Assyria, spared at Jonah's preaching.
The empire and city of Judah's exile.
The mount of the city of David; the beloved of God.
